title: [FIRRTL] Add UnknownValueOp
body:
Add an `UnknownValueOp` property expression.  This exists to workaround a
quirk of properties, their undocumented ABI, and their lowering.  An
unknown value expression represents a property value of a specific type
that is unknown and cannot be known, but also cannot be removed.

Consider a situation involving a public module which has input properties
and output properties.  This input/output relationship defines a
"function" that can be accessed by evaluating the MLIR after compilation
finishes.  However, the instantiator has no idea what to set for this
value as the intent of the properties is for a function _that the user can
call_.

While we have not seen a situation where this has arisen for properties,
yet, this has come up in the lowering of domains to properties.
Specifically, this is intended to be used as part of the lowering from
anonymous domains to anonymous OM values.  Full FIRRTL support, including
parsing and emission, is adding for completeness.
